steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Before the blank is an independent clause with an appositive phrase ('the site with the highest elevation'). After the blank is a second independent clause ('it had shifted'). The blank must join two independent clauses correctly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — elevation and: Joining two independent clauses with only a comma is a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice B — elevation: Embedding the conjunction here breaks the appositive and misreads the sentence structure. ✗
  • Choice C — elevation,: The punctuation does not properly close the appositive phrase before the boundary. ✗
  • Choice D — elevation, and: A comma closes the appositive, and 'and' (a coordinating conjunction) correctly joins the two independent clauses. ✓
That matches Choice D — elevation, and.
